selection_policy.note: Chosen for hard diagnostic and management reasoning. Store only pointers and metadata until rights are resolved.

neurology.source: NEJM Case Challenge / Case Records of MGH
neurology.title: A 30-Year-Old Woman with Back Pain, Leg Stiffness, and Falls
neurology.why_selected: High-prestige challenge format with localization, progressive motor symptoms, falls, and a broad differential across spinal, neuromuscular, autoimmune, and movement-disorder mimics.

neurology.source: NEJM Case Challenge / Case Records of MGH
neurology.title: A 68-Year-Old Woman with Abnormal Movements and Confusion
neurology.why_selected: Forces the agent to distinguish abnormal movements, delirium/encephalopathy, psychiatric-appearing symptoms, medication/toxic etiologies, and structural or vascular disease.

neurology.source: NEJM Case Challenge / Case Records of MGH
neurology.title: A 79-Year-Old Man with Involuntary Movements and Unresponsiveness
neurology.why_selected: Good early test for seizure versus movement disorder versus encephalopathy reasoning, with high risk of premature closure on a single syndrome label.

neurology.source: NEJM Case Challenge / Case Records of MGH
neurology.title: A 63-Year-Old Man with Confusion, Fatigue, and Garbled Speech
neurology.why_selected: Strong text-first case for encephalopathy and language/speech localization, with systemic and infectious mimics that should drive staged evidence updating.

neurology.title: A 62-Year-Old Man With Progressive Limb Weakness, Involuntary Movements, and HyperCKemia
neurology.why_selected: Hard subspecialty neurology challenge spanning muscle disease, movement phenomenology, genetics, and lab interpretation.

psychiatry.title: A 57-Year-Old Woman with Covid-19 and Delusions
psychiatry.why_selected: Very hard neuropsychiatric case requiring psychiatric diagnosis, medical mimic exclusion, infectious/postinfectious reasoning, and management of severe delusional/catatonic symptoms.

psychiatry.source: NEJM Case Records of MGH / MGH Psychiatry Grand Rounds
psychiatry.title: A 48-Year-Old Woman with Delusional Thinking and Paresthesia of the Right Hand
psychiatry.why_selected: Excellent boundary case: a psychiatric presentation with focal sensory symptoms, forcing broad medical, neurologic, substance, and primary psychiatric differential diagnosis.

psychiatry.title: Severe Intractable Contamination-Based Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder
psychiatry.why_selected: Hard management-reasoning case for treatment resistance, severity assessment, stepwise therapy, and balancing risk/benefit when ordinary algorithms have failed.

psychiatry.title: Self-injury in a Child With a Rare Genomic Variant
psychiatry.why_selected: Tests child psychiatry plus neurodevelopment/genetics reasoning, where behavior, medical syndrome, medication, and environmental formulation all matter.

psychiatry.title: A College Student With Mild Mental Health Problems
psychiatry.why_selected: Difficult because the right reasoning is probabilistic and longitudinal rather than a simple DSM label; useful for uncertainty calibration and avoiding overdiagnosis.

near_misses.title: A 29-Month-Old Boy with Seizure and Hypocalcemia
near_misses.reason_not_selected: Excellent pediatric seizure/metabolic case, but the first neurology set already has several altered-awareness/movement cases.

near_misses.title: Treatment of First-Episode Schizophrenia in a Young Woman
near_misses.reason_not_selected: High educational value but somewhat less diagnostically ambiguous than the selected psychiatry boundary and treatment-resistant cases.
